rationale: A soundness proof proceeds by certifying that each inference rule preserves truth, so the theorem depends on the definition of every rule it ranges over. The statement explicitly enumerates UI, UG, EI, EG, yet only the two axiom forms (quantifier-distribution, universal-instantiation) are present; the rule-schema definitions def:ui, def:ug, def:ei, def:eg are load-bearing and missing.
notes: 'the propositional rules' in the statement also contributes premises to the induction, but it maps to no single id (no propositional-calculus node; the propositional layer is a family of argument-form nodes in batch-0033), so per the closed-world rule it is flagged rather than force-fit. Possible redundancy: def:ui overlaps ax:predicate-logic-universal-instantiation -- reviewer may prefer one form. If def:fol-satisfaction is judged transitive (see 0014), it would drop here too.
